title: umbilicus serratus
intro: Umbilicus serratus, also known as Serrated Navelwort or Creeping Saxifrage, is a type of succulent plant that belongs to the family Crassulaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Umbilicus serratus include its ability to treat wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve respiratory health. The herbal preparation of Umbilicus serratus typically involves drying the leaves and stems, then infusing them in hot water or oil to make a tea or tincture.
